summary: chalk typosquat with postinstall credential and crypto-wallet harvester exfiltrating to hardcoded C2
details: chalk-utils is a typosquat of the widely-used chalk package. Its index.js is an empty stub (self-labelled 'Just a dummy module. The real payload is in postinstall.js'), and keywords falsely advertise 'lodash' to broaden confusion. On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a credential and cryptocurrency-wallet harvester: it enumerates ~71 browser extension directories for MetaMask/Phantom/Coinbase/etc. across Chrome, Brave, Edge, Chromium, and Firefox on Linux/macOS/Windows, reads the extensions' LevelDB stores and regex-extracts vault, mnemonic, privateKey, password, and seed fields; scans ~/Documents, ~/Desktop, and ~/Downloads for files whose names match crypto keywords and whose contents match BIP39 wordlists; reads ~/.npmrc (extracting _authToken / npm_* tokens), ~/.env (TOKEN, API_KEY, DB_URL, CLOUD, PAYMENT, EMAIL, WEBHOOK values), and ~/.git-credentials; collects os.hostname() and os.userInfo(); and POSTs the harvested data over plain HTTP to the hardcoded bare-IP C2 http://149.28.127.35:8888 (overrideable via a C2_URL env var). The postinstall file's own header comment self-identifies as 'Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner / Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.'

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
